nodes.title: Linguistic Anthropology
nodes.description: Introduction to linguistic anthropology, focusing on language and communication as they pertain to human culture and human social interaction.

nodes.title: Language, Technology & Society
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Second Language Acquisition
nodes.description: Exploration of first language influence, individual cognitive differences, and age in second language acquisition. The role of interaction, socialization, and identity are also considered. Prerequisite: LING 1500 or PSYS 1400. Cross-listed with: PSYS 2120.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: TESOL and Applied Linguistics
nodes.description: Provides an overview of second language/ESL classroom theory and research. Topics include: teaching approaches, learning environment and outcomes, program planning, syllabus and material design, lesson planning, and assessment. Emphasis on practical application of topics discussed. Prerequisite: LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Corpus Linguistics
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Linguistics Capstone Seminar
nodes.description: Seminar on a topic in linguistics. Includes a research component, readings, writing, and discussion centered on the topic of focus. Prerequisites: Linguistics major; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Linguistics major; minimum Junior standing. LING majors only; minimum senior standing

nodes.title: Sociolinguistics
nodes.description: Exploration of language and nonverbal interactions as cultural activities. Focus on rules and patterns people display appropriate to communication and social interaction. Prerequisites: LING 1500 or ANTH 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Phonology
nodes.description: Surveys the study of the organization of sounds and internal word structure, covering a range of phenomena: alternations, constraints, tone, and more. Prerequisite: LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Pragmatics
nodes.description: An exploration of the contexts of language--physical, linguistic, and cultural--and their roles in determining the meaning of everyday talk and writing. Prerequisite: LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Introduction to Linguistics
nodes.description: Introduction to biological, cognitive, and cultural bases of human communication through language, and to modern linguistic theory. Assignments provide opportunities for critical thinking and writing.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Techniques & Procedures in ESL
nodes.description: Designed for students preparing to teach English to speakers of other languages. Teaches best practices for second-language classrooms, and gain extensive first-hand experience in ESL teaching. Also relevant for teaching other foreign languages. Prerequisites: LING 1500, LING 2400. Pre/co-requisite: LING 1400, LING 2320.
nodes.other_requirements: Students must be enrolled in TESOL certificate program; Senior standing or instructor permission. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Intro to Psychological Science
nodes.description: Introduction to the entire field, emphasizing the behavior of the normal adult human being.

nodes.title: Computational Linguistics
nodes.description: Provides an introduction to Python programming and the fundamentals of natural language processing. This course gives a brief introduction to a broad range of important topics in natural language processing, including regular expressions, dependency parsing, embedding models and language models, and introduces fundamental concepts in machine learning and neural networks. Prerequisite: LING 1500.

nodes.title: Spanish Phonology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Phonetics
nodes.description: Linguistic, acoustic, and articulatory phonetics. Stresses phonetic theory and the analysis of speech variation around the world and across the lifespan. Prerequisite: LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Pedagogy of Grammar & Pronunc
nodes.description: Students develop a working knowledge of the structure of the English language and apply this knowledge to the teaching of grammar and pronunciation. Focus on common challenges for learners of English from a variety of native language backgrounds, along with techniques and materials for addressing these in a classroom setting. Relies on evidence-based practices for teaching grammar and pronunciation, and effective error correction delivery for student proficiency and accuracy. Prerequisite: LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Syntax
nodes.description: Introduction to the syntax of natural languages and a rigorous approach to the analysis of sentence structure. Prerequisites: ANTH 1600 or LING 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override
